name: Revolution Day (Cuba)
traditions: Rally at Revolution Square; speeches; cultural performances; military displays; community celebrations.
historicalBackground: Revolution Day marks January 1, 1959, when Fidel Castro's 26th of July Movement overthrew the Batista government after years of guerrilla warfare.
description: Cuba's celebration of the 1959 Revolution, with rallies and cultural events at Havana's iconic Revolution Square.
